claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 8.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 is congruent to 1 modulo 3.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n ≡ ±1 (mod 3) and n ≡ ±1 (mod 8).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 3) or ((n % 3 == 1 or n % 3 == 2) and (n % 8 == 1 or n % 8 == 7))

claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n is odd.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 ≡ 1 (mod 8) and n^2 ≡ 1 (mod 2).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^4 ≡ 1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^6 ≡ 1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 24 divides n^2 - 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 24 divides (n - 1)(n + 1).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 12 divides n^2 - 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n is odd and n^2 - 1 is divisible by 12.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 ≡ 1 (mod 4) and n^2 ≡ 1 (mod 3).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n - 1 is even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n + 1 is even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then both n - 1 and n + 1 are even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 4 divides (n - 1)(n + 1).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n ≡ 1 or 5 (mod 6).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^3 ≡ n (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^5 ≡ n (mod 120).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then gcd(n^4 - 1, n^6 - 1) divides 24.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 3) or (gcd(pow(n, 4) - 1, pow(n, 6) - 1) != 0 and pow(n, 4, 24) == 1 and pow(n, 6, 24) == 1)

claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n ≡ 1 (mod 6) or n ≡ 5 (mod 6).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^12 ≡ 1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 ≡ 0 (mod 24) and n^3 - n ≡ 0 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n ≡ ±1 (mod 3).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n ≡ ±1 (mod 8).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n belongs to one of 8 residue classes modulo 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^8 ≡ 1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 = (n-1)(n+1) where both factors are even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then one of n-1 or n+1 is divisible by 4.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then the sum (n-1) + (n+1) = 2n is divisible by 2 but not 4.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 3) or (2 * n % 2 == 0 and 2 * n % 4 == 2)

claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then at least one of n-1, n+1 is divisible by 4.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 2 divides n^2 - 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 3 divides n^2 - 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then 6 divides n^2 - 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 + 1 is even.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 2 ≡ -1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n(n^2 - 1) ≡ 0 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^4 ≡ 1 (mod 24) and n^8 ≡ 1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then phi(24) divides the order of n modulo 24.
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 3) or (phi(24) >= 1 and pow(n, phi(24), 24) == 1)

claim: For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^2 - 1 is divisible by both 6 and 12, hence by their lcm.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then sigma(n) > n and sigma(n) = n + 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then phi(n) = n - 1.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then tau(n) = 2.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^(phi(n)) ≡ 1 (mod 24).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then (n-1)^2 ≡ 1 (mod 24) implies n^(n-1) has specific structure mod 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then n^5 - n ≡ 0 (mod 120).
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then (n-1)(n+1) and n(n^2-1) share common divisibility structure with 24.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then exactly 8 residue classes modulo 24 contain all such primes.
For all integers n, if n is prime and n > 3 then both n^12 ≡ 1 (mod 24) and n^(n-1) ≡ 1 (mod 24).
predicate: (not is_prime(n)) or (n <= 3) or (pow(n, 12, 24) == 1 and pow(n, n - 1, 24) == 1)
